name: Axis taxonomy and target-class mapping
how_it_solves: Formal enum CandidateAxis at api/services/synthetic_lethality/v3/multimodal/models.py:122 with 7 axes. Drug-to-axis mapping table at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66.
why_it_exists: Encoded gap between CrisPRO methodology and Bev2026.

attributes.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on biology (correct axis assignment). CrisPRO has the framework but a fixable bug in the mapping.

raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_approach: Paper introduces 'rational drug combinations' across 4 mechanism axes: nucleoside analogs (cytarabine, gemcitabine), antifolates (MTX), PRMT5 inhibitors (MRTX1719) + MAT2A inhibitors (AG-270), ATR/CHK1/WEE1 inhibitors (VX970, AZD7762, MK-1775). No formal enum.
raw_dimension_record.CrisPRO_approach: Formal enum CandidateAxis at api/services/synthetic_lethality/v3/multimodal/models.py:122 with 7 axes. Drug-to-axis mapping table at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66.

CrisPRO_known_gap.correct_mapping_per_Bev2026_and_Engstrom: 'mrtx1719': 'prmt5_mtap_cooperative' (a new axis that does not yet exist in the CandidateAxis enum)
CrisPRO_known_gap.evidence_strength: TRIPLE-SOURCE: Bev2026 §Results + Methods (MRTX1719 = PRMT5 inhibitor); Engstrom 2023 Cancer Discov 13:2412-2431 (Bev2026 ref 7) is the originating paper defining MRTX1719 as MTA-cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor; Pettus 2025 J Med Chem 68:6932-6954 (Bev2026 ref 8) confirms MTA-cooperative PRMT5 axis for AMG 193.
CrisPRO_known_gap.remediation_required: Add PRMT5_MTAP_COOPERATIVE to CandidateAxis enum; remap mrtx1719 and add AMG 193 / pemrametostat to same axis. Out of scope per user lock #4.
CrisPRO_known_gap.impact_on_demichelis_outreach: Demichelis lab will immediately notice this bug if shown the current taxonomy. Pre-outreach fix recommended.

raw_dimension_record.moat_status: Bev2026 ADVANTAGE on biology (correct axis assignment). CrisPRO has the framework but a fixable bug in the mapping.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_verbatim: First, we investigated the activity of the MAT2A inhibitor AG-270 and the PRMT5 inhibitor MRTX1719, which selectively binds to PRMT5 complexed with MTA, to define the ideal dose for a clear genotype-specific effect when administered alone in our cell models.
raw_dimension_record.Bev2026_evidence_verbatim_source: Bev2026 PDF, Results section header 'Cytarabine synergizes with PRMT5 and MAT2A inhibitors selectively in bladder cancer cells with MTAP loss' (page 5, first paragraph)
raw_dimension_record.Engstrom_2023_verbatim: Engstrom, L. D. et al. MRTX1719 is an MTA-cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor that exhibits synthetic lethality in preclinical models and patients with MTAP-deleted cancer. Cancer Discov. 13, 2412–2431 (2023).
raw_dimension_record.Pettus_2025_verbatim: Pettus, L. H. et al. Discovery of AMG 193, an MTA-cooperative PRMT5 inhibitor for the treatment of MTAP-deleted cancers. J. Med. Chem. 68, 6932–6954 (2025).
